what_it_does: A territory-organized file map of the counterpart/design repo, derived as the 'MAP layer' by a haiku swarm, listing ~267 files with objective, one-line-to-paragraph summaries; it flags three machinery clusters (decision-card, generative-render, graph-instrument) and walks each file's contents and relationships.
observations: Derived by a 'haiku swarm' with one agent per ≤5 files, covering 267 files.
Top section flags three clusters: decision-card (11 files), generative-render (119 files), graph-instrument (14 files).
Files are grouped by territory: / (185 entries), app/ (7), archive/ (17), assets/ (1), docs/ (5), engine/ (8), factory/ (2), pieces/ (12), probes/ (2), registry/ (1), surface/ (27).
Each entry follows a consistent pattern: path · type [optional cluster tag] — one-line is; contains: ...; relates: ...
Some paths are absolute (/home/tim/repos/counterpart/design/...) while others are relative.
Entries include both hand-authored docs and generated outputs (e.g., factory/output/*.json, surface/styles/surface.css).
The map explicitly avoids judgment and ranking, presenting itself as objective facts only.
Decision-card and graph-instrument tags link specific docs and code files (e.g., docs/command/DECISION-SEQUENCE-SURFACE-DESIGN.md, surface/runtime/unit-view.js, app/graph.html).
The root territory is dominated by dna/*.json registries, docs/*.md design docs, and pieces/*.html mockups.
standouts: 119 files tagged generative-render versus only 11 decision-card and 14 graph-instrument files, showing where the bulk of machinery lives.
The file is both a navigation map and an interpretive ledger, with per-file 'contains' and 'relates' summaries.
Its stated mission is to help Tim locate the real generative/decision-card code so decision cards can be rebuilt from the actual system.
conventions: Each file entry uses the pattern: path · kind [cluster] — summary; contains: bullet list; relates: bullet list.
Cluster tags appear in italics with square brackets, e.g., _[generative-render]_.
Uses '·' as a separator and '—' for em-dashes.
Mixes absolute and relative path styles within the same list.
Descriptions are factual and avoid evaluative language, per the 'no judgment' rule.
concerns: Path inconsistency (absolute vs relative) may hurt portability or automated parsing.
The 185-entry root section is very long and not further subdivided, making scanning harder.
Cluster criteria are not defined in the file; it is unclear why a file gets one tag versus another or whether tags can overlap.
Despite claiming 'objective facts, no judgment', phrases like 'demonstrates' and 'relates' carry light interpretation.
notes: A dense, territory-organized inventory of the design repo that tags every file by function and clusters the generative/render/decision-card/graph machinery for Tim's review.
questions: What exactly is a 'haiku swarm' and how were the per-file summaries produced?
Are the cluster counts mutually exclusive, or can a file belong to multiple clusters?
Why are some paths absolute and others relative?
What are the precise criteria for tagging a file as decision-card versus generative-render versus graph-instrument?
How current is this map relative to the live repo state?
What is the 'MAP layer' in the broader layer model referenced in the title?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to take Tim through what exists and find the generative/decision-card code to redo decision cards; actual content is a comprehensive, territory-organized file ledger with machinery clusters. It matches the stated purpose while also serving as a broader repo index.
intention_signals: To create a readable, searchable ledger of a large design-code estate so a human reviewer can quickly locate the generative rendering, decision-card, and graph-instrument machinery before redesigning decision cards.
novelty: The 'haiku swarm' derivation and 'MAP layer' framing, plus combining high-level machinery flags with per-file haiku-like summaries and typed 'contains/relates' metadata in a single markdown ledger.
contribution: Provides a human-navigable index and machinery-cluster tags for a large, versioned design system repo, helping identify which files participate in generative rendering, decision cards, and graph instruments without making comparative judgments.
summary_for_embedding: A territory-organized file map of the counterpart/design repo listing ~267 files with objective one-line summaries, contains/relates metadata, and flagged clusters for generative-render, decision-card, and graph-instrument machinery.
intention_for_embedding: To give Tim a readable inventory of the existing design-code estate and surface the files involved in generative rendering, decision cards, and graph instruments for a redesign effort.
